channel.title: Glass Empires SRD
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Glass Empires SRD

item.title: { 3 } - Character Creation
item.description: This procedure creates a Player Character embedded in Glass’s factional landscape.

item.title: { 2 } - Campaigns @
item.description: @ How to minimally start a game.

item.title: { 6 } - Marketplace
item.description: A marketplace is a gathering of people offering goods & services within a given domain.

item.title: { 4 } - Factions
item.description: A Faction is one or more characters that compose a set of dependent & supportive Families surrounding a place of operations called a Capital, working under a notable or charismatic Leader (or Ruler) for the sake of achieving a shared agenda, represented by a series of Moves set & achieved on...

item.title: { 8 } - Armies
item.description: @ Axioms Aia is a flat discworld & the implications of this has a radical impact on military operations, which renders the entirety of the real world’s strategic & tactical doctrine incomplete, misleading, or entirely obsolete.

item.title: { 7 } - Adventure
item.description: @ This playmode is applicable to when you’re adjudicating the adventure & movement of a handful of characters through dangerous environments.

item.title: { 15 } - Minor Powers @
item.description: @ Gods of Glass, Imperial Family, Papermark, Theomni, Kain & the Oropolis, Mountain Imps. The secondary players and wildcards.

item.title: { 12 } - The Island of Glass @
item.description: @ Geography, natural resources, geodestiny, neighbors (Venetian League, Hydrea, Coralers, Boreal Coast, Cyclopean Continent).

item.title: Glass Empires SRD
item.description: Glass Empires Glass Empires is a tabletop RPG about pre-industrial power dynamics on an animistic discworld — faction-building, debt, rulership, and the nature of the divine.

item.title: { 4 } - Encounters & Locations @
item.description: @ Locations You should create a 1d6 random table of at least 6 distinct Locations for each distinct area, biome, or region in play.
